key_insight: OpenProver combines an LLM planner, parallel workers, and Lean 4 verification to make theorem proving more interactive, reproducible, and automatically checked.
engineering_relevance: The system provides a practical open-source workflow for building and evaluating agentic proof assistants with human-in-the-loop steering and formal verification.
method: Planner maintains a compact Whiteboard and an unbounded Repository of intermediate results.
Decomposes proof search into parallel Workers coordinated by the Planner.
Verifies generated proofs automatically in Lean 4 and supports terminal-based interactive guidance.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluate OpenProver on ProofNet and compare it against a simple baseline, enabled by automatic formal verification for quantitative ablations.
watch_item: Performance claims may depend on Lean 4 verification coverage and the quality of the planning/search decomposition, which can limit scalability on harder proofs.
paper_title: OpenProver: Agentic and Interactive Theorem Proving with Lean 4
